title: Japanese Parliament remains paralyzed over Imperial House Law reform
summary: Government and opposition accuse each other of blocking the resumption of sessions, while the prime minister expresses his displeasure.

title: Japanese 10-year bond yield rises to 2.81%, highest in 30 years
summary: The rise reflects expectations of accelerated inflation and possible monetary tightening.

title: Wages in Japan rise 5.01% in spring, third consecutive year with 5% increases
summary: Small and medium-sized enterprises also record record increases, albeit slightly below the previous year.

title: Moriyasu says Japan is on track to be number one despite World Cup elimination
summary: The Japanese national team coach defends his management and sparks a wave of reactions in South Korea.

title: Japan reaches record tax revenue of 84 trillion yen in 2025
summary: The figure, nine trillion higher than the previous year, increases pressure to return the surplus to taxpayers.

title: Japan plans to drastically increase fees for residence permits from October
summary: More than 90% of asylum seekers would be excluded from reductions, according to the immigration agency directive.

title: LDP proposes expanding communications interception to strengthen intelligence
summary: The ruling party presents a draft that includes administrative surveillance as a security tool.

title: Reiwa Shinsengumi leader Taro Yamamoto fined for speeding
summary: The lawmaker was caught at 149 km/h on a highway, resulting in a fine and license suspension.

title: Millions of Iranians attend funeral of Ayatollah Khamenei and his granddaughter
summary: Images of the Supreme Leader's coffin and the little girl shock the world.

title: Man rescued 8 days after earthquake in Venezuela that left more than 2,500 dead
summary: The discovery of a survivor among the rubble is described as a miracle.

title: Tokyo celebrates 250th anniversary of US independence with fireworks and presence of Japanese authorities
summary: The event included a message from former President Trump and the participation of Governor Koike.

title: Five years after the Atami landslide, inadequate landfills persist throughout Japan
summary: The central government and municipalities seek to share successful cases of unstable soil removal.

title: Father arrested for death of his wife and daughter in Nagano; victims died of suffocation
summary: The autopsy revealed that the mother and 12-year-old girl died by strangulation.

title: Japan Airlines plane makes emergency landing at Kansai airport due to oil leak
summary: No injuries were reported, but the runway was temporarily closed for inspection.

title: Talent Bobby Ologun prosecuted for rape in Chiba
summary: The prosecution accuses him of engaging in non-consensual sexual relations under the pretext of a massage.

title: Former world boxing champion Yasuei Yakushiji prosecuted for falsifying adoption registration
summary: The boxer allegedly attempted to adopt his lover without his wife's permission.

title: Baseball player Akira Nakamura announces retirement from SoftBank Hawks
summary: In his final game, teammate Hiroshi Kawasaki presented him with sunflowers and hit the game-winning hit.

title: Japan plans to double railway capacity to Narita airport by the 2030s
summary: A new express train will connect Haneda and Narita to accommodate passenger growth due to airport expansion.

title: Emperors Naruhito and Masako report to the Emperor Emeritus on their official trip to the Netherlands and Belgium
summary: The imperial couple made a state visit that included meetings with European royalty.

title: Shintaro Mochizuki and Naomi Osaka advance to the round of 16 at Wimbledon
summary: Mochizuki reaches the second week of a Grand Slam for the first time, while Osaka shines again in the tournament.
